4. Supports landscape/portrait with "_H" and "_S" animation naming

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>FreeGame entrance animation controller implemented</title>
  <subtitle>Created FreeGameIntro component with event-driven In-Loop-Out animation sequencing and landscape/portrait support</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FreeGameIntro.ts created in assets/Script/FreeGame/ with InLoopOutSpine property for spine animation control</fact>
    <fact>FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY event triggers PlayIntro method with optional callback parameter</fact>
    <fact>Animation sequence executes In animation, loops indefinitely, waits configurable duration (default 2s), then plays Out animation</fact>
    <fact>Landscape animations use "_H" suffix (In_H, Loop_H, Out_H) and portrait uses "_S" suffix (In_S, Loop_S, Out_S)</fact>
    <fact>Orientation detection uses GameView.IsLandscape via Common.GetGameView() to select appropriate animation set</fact>
    <fact>m_loopDuration property exposes configurable wait time between Loop start and Out animation trigger</fact>
    <fact>Error handling includes try-catch block with ForceStop() call and isPlaying guard to prevent concurrent playback</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented FreeGameIntro component as an event-driven controller for FreeGame entrance animations. The component uses composition rather than inheritance, exposing an InLoopOutSpine property that can be wired up in the Cocos Creator editor. The animation flow follows the requested In→Loop→Wait→Out pattern: PlayIn() awaits entrance completion, PlayLoop() starts looping without await, Define.Wait() pauses for the configurable duration, then PlayOut() completes the sequence. Orientation handling dynamically selects "_H" or "_S" suffixed animation names by checking GameView.IsLandscape at playback time. The event-driven architecture using FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY matches existing patterns like FREE_GAME_YOU_WIN_EVENT, allowing other game states to trigger the intro via eventManager.Dispatch. Error handling ensures ForceStop() is called if animation playback fails, and the isPlaying flag prevents overlapping sequences.</narrative>
